text_blocks.text: Managers in the Premier League
are involved in the day-to-day running of
the team, including the training, team
selection, and player acquisition. Their
influence varies from club-to-club and is
related to the ownership of the club and the
relationship of the manager with fans.
Managers are required to have a UEFA Pro
Licence which is the final coaching
qualification available, and follows the
completion of the UEFA 'B' and 'A' Licences.
The UEFA Pro Licence is required by every
person who wishes to manage a club in the
Premier League on a permanent basis (i.e.
more than 12 weeks - the amount of time
an unqualified caretaker manager is allowed to
take control). Caretaker appointments are
managers that fill the gap between a
managerial departure and a new appointment.
Several caretaker managers have gone on to
secure a permanent managerial post after
performing well as a caretaker; examples
include Paul Hart at Portsmouth and David
Pleat at Tottenham Hotspur.
